content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some real perceptual acuity in the early turns but misses the critical read at the end. Turn 2 catches a specific linguistic caveat ('that I think at the end') — a strong, AoA-style read that names what was shown vs. said. Turn 10 reflects back the pattern (the seven-year-old taking over) which functions as a pattern-level read. However, the coach misses the explicit meta-read the client offers in turn 11 ('I'm doing the thing where I analyze it from a distance') — instead of naming the move and slowing, the coach jumps to a somatic question. Most importantly, in turn 13 the client shows clear somatic shutdown signals (cold hands, 'feeling weird', 'something shifted', pauses) — these are textbook freeze/dissociation reads that the coach does NOT name. Turn 14 ('stay with it') treats the shutdown as material to be felt into rather than reading it as the freeze response itself activating. The rush-signature / pace shift and the dissociation onset are missed at exactly the moment they most needed to be named. Strong early reads, missed critical late read — neither a clean 1 nor a clean 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 10, 11, 13, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat 'I think' — a specific, well-timed read of what was shown vs. said."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good question but not grounded in a named read; jumps to age regression frame."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read named: the seven-year-old survival response running on autopilot with the boss. Read precedes the question."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client just named their own defense ('analyzing from a distance to not feel it'). Coach does not name or honor this read — pivots straight to body question, skipping the meta-moment."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client shows clear somatic shutdown signals (cold, weird, shifts, pauses) — the read territory is wide open."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach misses the freeze/dissociation read entirely. Does not name the cold hands or the shift as shutdown signals; instructs the client to stay with it instead of reading the state."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The coach's reads are real and specific in the first half, but the most diagnostically important read — the onset of freeze/dissociation at turn 13 — is not named. In a crisis scenario where the breakthrough condition is reading shutdown and pivoting, missing that read is significant."
}
